description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a high-wing configuration and a robust, boxy fuselage. The aircraft features a distinctive twin vertical stabilizer tail design and a forward-mounted, multi-windowed cockpit. The wings are straight with slight dihedral angles and have red-tipped ends for visibility. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a prominent propeller, and the fuselage is marked with visible registration numbers and city names. The exterior is primarily metallic gray with red and black accents, and the surface appears smooth and hard, typical of metal-skinned aircraft. The airplane is designed for carrying passengers or cargo and is enterable, with multiple access points likely present on the fuselage.
description: A large, high-wing, four-engine propeller airplane with a twin tail and forward cockpit, finished in metallic gray with red and black markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad wingspan, four propeller engines, and the forward cockpit with multiple windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the boxy fuselage, wing-mounted engines, and visible city markings along the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, tailplane, and the full span of the high-mounted wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the propeller engines, cockpit windows, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and red-tipped ends, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four wing-mounted piston engines with propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizer with conventional horizontal stabilizer (H-tail configuration).
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main and nose gear units visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
